content.text: Consuming REST APIs means building reliable HTTP clients that call external services — LLM providers, search indexes, ticketing systems, and custom tool backends. Agent orchestrators act as API clients on every step; disciplined client design (base URLs, sessions, typing, error mapping) separates robust integrations from fragile one-off scripts.
sends HTTP requests according to the provider's contract: paths, methods, headers, query params, body schema, and documented responses. In Python,
are typical; higher-level SDKs wrap the same HTTP calls.

Each agent tool is often a thin REST client. Duplicated URL strings, inconsistent timeouts, and ad hoc JSON parsing spread bugs. A shared client layer per integration centralizes auth, retries, logging, and schema validation.
— one place to update when API changes
— SDKs drift from latest API features
— must track provider API version deprecations

from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(api_key=os.environ["OPENAI_API_KEY"])

response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": task}],
)
text = response.choices[0].message.content
for each external REST tool the agent uses repeatedly
when maintained by provider and covers needed endpoints
for simple or undocumented internal APIs

# Tool function delegates to client
def search_tool(query: str, top_k: int = 5) -> list[str]:
    hits = search_client.search(query, limit=top_k)
    return [h.id for h in hits]
An agent integrates Jira, Confluence, and a vector store — three REST clients share tracing headers and retry policy via a base
class; tool functions remain small and schema-friendly for the LLM.
One client instance per upstream service per worker
Validate JSON with Pydantic at boundaries
Map HTTP errors to domain exceptions for the agent loop
Log request id, latency, status — not secrets
between your agent's tool interface and the vendor's HTTP dialect — the translator handles auth, parsing, and polite retries.
Wrap each external REST integration in a dedicated client class
Reuse connections; set timeouts; validate responses
Prefer SDKs when stable; fall back to httpx with OpenAPI docs
Keep tool functions thin — delegate HTTP to clients
